truth.papers.A35.title: Improving impact toughness of Fe–20Mn–9Al-1.5C–2Ni–3Cr low-density steel by optimizing grain boundaries via multi-stage heat treatment without compromising high strength and ductility
truth.papers.A35.paper_scope: Establish the relationship between microstructure and mechanical properties of Fe–20Mn–9Al-1.5C–2Ni–3Cr low-density steel, and investigate key microstructural characteristics that influence crack propagation resistance during impact while maintaining high strength.

prediction.papers.A35.title: Improving impact toughness of Fe–20Mn–9Al-1.5C–2Ni–3Cr low-density steel by optimizing grain boundaries via multi-stage heat treatment without compromising high strength and ductility
prediction.papers.A35.paper_scope: Investigating the relationship between microstructure and mechanical properties of Fe-Mn-Al-C-Ni-Cr low-density steel, focusing on improving impact toughness via multi-stage heat treatment through grain boundary engineering while maintaining high strength and ductility.

truth.alloys.alloy_fe20mn9al15c2ni3cr.alloys_n: Analyzed composition: Fe-20.3Mn-8.6Al-1.4C-2.1Ni-2.9Cr (wt.%). Density 6.78 g/cm³ (14% lower than pure iron).

prediction.alloys.alloy_fe20mn9al15c2ni3cr.alloys_n: Analyzed composition Fe-20.3Mn-8.6Al-1.4C-2.1Ni-2.9Cr in wt.%. Density measured by drainage method is 6.78 g/cm³, about 14% lower than pure iron (7.87 g/cm³). Ingot homogenized at 1200°C for 2 h then hot-rolled at 1050–950°C to 6 mm sheet followed by air cooling.

truth.processes.proc_s1000.description: Homogenized at 1200 °C for 2 h, hot-rolled at 1050-950 °C to 6 mm sheet, air cooled, then single-stage heat treatment at 1000 °C for 3 min followed by quenching to room temperature.
truth.processes.proc_s1050.description: Homogenized at 1200 °C for 2 h, hot-rolled at 1050-950 °C to 6 mm sheet, air cooled, then single-stage heat treatment at 1050 °C for 3 min followed by quenching to room temperature.
truth.processes.proc_s1100.description: Homogenized at 1200 °C for 2 h, hot-rolled at 1050-950 °C to 6 mm sheet, air cooled, then single-stage heat treatment at 1100 °C for 3 min followed by quenching to room temperature. Used only for comparison of tensile properties.
truth.processes.proc_mht.description: Homogenized at 1200 °C for 2 h, hot-rolled at 1050-950 °C to 6 mm sheet, air cooled, then multi-stage heat treatment: first stage at 1000 °C for 3 min + quench, second stage at 750 °C for 15 min + quench, third stage at 1050 °C for 3 min + quench to room temperature.

prediction.processes.proc_s1000.description: Single-stage heat treatment at 1000°C for 3 min followed by quenching to room temperature (S1000).
prediction.processes.proc_s1000.processes_notes: Shared pre-processing: homogenization at 1200°C for 2 h, hot-rolling at 1050–950°C to 6 mm sheet, air cooling to room temperature.
prediction.processes.proc_s1050.description: Single-stage heat treatment at 1050°C for 3 min followed by quenching to room temperature (S1050).
prediction.processes.proc_s1100.description: Single-stage heat treatment at 1100°C for 3 min followed by quenching to room temperature (S1100).
prediction.processes.proc_s1100.processes_notes: Used for tensile property comparison only.
prediction.processes.proc_mht.description: Multi-stage heat treatment (MHT): first stage at 1000°C for 3 min quenched, second stage at 750°C for 15 min quenched, third stage at 1050°C for 3 min quenched.

truth.processing_steps.sample_s1100.processing: Single-stage heat treatment S1100, used for comparison of tensile properties only.

truth.structures.sample_s1000.overall_structur: Austenite matrix with grain boundary Cr7C3 precipitates and nano-scale κ-carbides
truth.structures.sample_s1000.microstructure_l: homogeneously within austenite grains, co-gridded with austenite
truth.structures.sample_s1050.overall_structur: Austenite matrix with nano-scale κ-carbides and B2 precipitates; no Cr7C3
truth.structures.sample_s1100.overall_structur: Austenite matrix with coarser grains, expected κ-carbides and possibly B2 (not detailed)
truth.structures.sample_mht.overall_structure: Austenite matrix with nano-scale κ-carbides and B2 precipitates (slightly smaller than in S1050); no intergranular Cr7C3. Higher fraction of special grain boundaries.
truth.structures.sample_mht.microstructure_lis: equiaxed grains with annealing twins, higher twin density
in austenite, size slightly smaller than S1050

prediction.structures.sample_s1000.overall_structur: Austenite matrix with intergranular Cr7C3 precipitates and coherent nanoscale κ-carbides
prediction.structures.sample_s1000.microstructure_l: 3.17 μm (effective grain size including twin boundaries)
elongated stripe-like precipitates along austenite grain boundaries
prediction.structures.sample_s1050.overall_structur: Austenite matrix with dissolved intergranular precipitates, B2 phase and κ-carbides
prediction.structures.sample_s1050.microstructure_l: 3.84 μm (effective grain size including twin boundaries)
prediction.structures.sample_mht.overall_structure: Austenite matrix with dissolved intergranular precipitates, B2 phase and κ-carbides
prediction.structures.sample_mht.microstructure_lis: equiaxed grains with increased number of smaller annealing twins
7.31 μm (effective grain size including twin boundaries)
prediction.structures.sample_s1100.overall_structur: Austenite matrix (inferred from process; microstructural details not explicitly reported)

truth.interfaces.sample_s1000.defect_interacti: crack propagation along random high-angle boundaries, limited by special boundaries
Cracks nucleate at Cr7C3 precipitates on grain boundaries and propagate rapidly along RHAGBs; special boundaries have limited effect on crack deflection.
truth.interfaces.sample_s1000.phase_evolution: During quenching after 1000°C, Cr7C3 precipitates form on grain boundaries.
truth.interfaces.sample_s1050.phases[austenite: mix of special grain boundaries (~68.3%) and RHAGBs
truth.interfaces.sample_s1050.defect_interacti: crack propagation partially inhibited by special boundaries; mixed intergranular and transgranular
Absence of intergranular Cr7C3 delays crack nucleation; cracks follow special boundaries and are deflected, leading to higher propagation energy than S1000.
mix of special grain boundaries (~71.2%) and RHAGBs; increased number of twins
truth.interfaces.sample_mht.defect_interaction: crack propagation strongly hindered by special boundaries; zigzag cracking, increased propagation path
Larger grain size and higher frequency of special boundaries cause cracks to constantly cross or deflect along special boundaries, consuming more energy.
truth.interfaces.sample_mht.phase_evolution: Intermediate heat treatment at 750°C causes grain boundary precipitation, providing more twin nucleation sites during final 1050°C stage, leading to higher special boundary fraction.

prediction.interfaces.sample_s1000.phases[austenite: Σ3 coherent twin boundary (special boundary, low energy)
prediction.interfaces.sample_s1000.defect_interacti: crack propagation along random high-angle grain boundaries

Cracks nucleate preferentially at grain boundaries due to Cr7C3 precipitates and propagate along RHAGBs; special boundaries have limited effect on changing crack propagation or deflection because of intergranular precipitates.
prediction.interfaces.sample_s1000.interface_notes[: Slip bands on quasi-cleavage planes observed in impact fracture; microcracks and microvoids observed where slip band interaction is significant.
prediction.interfaces.sample_s1000.phase_evolution: Cr7C3 precipitates present along austenite grain boundaries; special boundary proportion 71.7%.
prediction.interfaces.sample_s1050.phases[austenite: Σ3 coherent twin boundary (special boundary, low energy)
prediction.interfaces.sample_s1050.defect_interacti: Elimination of intergranular precipitates allows special boundaries to interfere with RHAGBs; cracks propagate along or are deflected by special boundaries, producing mixed intergranular and transgranular fracture.
prediction.interfaces.sample_s1050.interface_notes[: Quasi-cleavage planes with slip bands present in impact fracture; ductile features increase compared with S1000.
prediction.interfaces.sample_s1050.phase_evolution: Cr7C3 dissolves at 1050°C; B2 phase precipitates within austenite; special boundary proportion decreases to 68.3%.
Σ3 coherent twin boundary (special boundary, low energy)
prediction.interfaces.sample_mht.defect_interaction: crack deflection and zigzag propagation across special boundaries

Larger grain size and higher frequency of special boundaries impede dislocations from crack tip; crack paths constantly cross special boundaries, are deflected and propagate in zigzag manner, increasing effective crack length and energy dissipation.
prediction.interfaces.sample_mht.interface_notes[1]: Impact fracture shows ductile dimple morphology; no quasi-cleavage planes; microcracks disappear.
prediction.interfaces.sample_mht.phase_evolution: Multi-stage treatment dissolves intergranular Cr7C3; intermediate 750°C stage promotes twin nucleation; final stage yields 71.2% special boundaries with larger grain size.

truth.properties.sample_s1000.mechanical.tensi: Engineering stress-strain and true stress-strain curves available in Fig.4

prediction.performance.sample_s1000.service_conditi: uniaxial tension and Charpy V-notch impact at ambient temperature
prediction.performance.sample_s1050.service_conditi: uniaxial tension and Charpy V-notch impact at ambient temperature
prediction.performance.sample_mht.service_condition: uniaxial tension and Charpy V-notch impact at ambient temperature

truth.characterization_methods.sample_s1000.ke: Total impact energy 29.2 J; crack initiation ~19.3 J, propagation ~9.9 J.
truth.characterization_methods.sample_s1050.ke: κ-carbides (3-10 nm) and B2 particles (20-160 nm) present; SAED patterns confirm superlattice spots.
truth.characterization_methods.sample_mht.key_: κ-carbides (3-10 nm) and B2 particles present; B2 size slightly smaller than in S1050.

prediction.characterization_methods.sample_s1000.ke: Quasi-cleavage planes with slip bands; microcracks and microvoids; apparent grain boundaries with no obvious deformation indicating intergranular fracture characteristics.
prediction.characterization_methods.sample_s1050.ke: Quasi-cleavage planes and slip bands; mixed intergranular and transgranular fracture mode.
prediction.characterization_methods.sample_mht.key_: Dimples increase and microcracks disappear; no quasi-cleavage planes; ductile fracture morphology.
prediction.characterization_methods.sample_s1100.pu: Mechanical property evaluation at room temperature

truth.computational_details.idx_0.validation_a: Calculated yield strengths compared with experimental values; contribution of solid solution, grain boundary, and precipitation strengthening evaluated.
truth.computational_details.idx_0.analytical_m: σ0 = 97 + 279*(wt% C) + 1.5*(wt% Mn) + 20.5*(wt% Al) + 2.9*(wt% Ni) + 3.9*(wt% Cr) MPa; σGR = K * d_eff^(-1/2) with K=461 MPa µm^(1/2); σPPT = σYS - σ0 - σGR
Solid solution strengthening calculated for each heat treatment condition using measured austenite compositions. C content calculated via Eq.(1) and (2).
Austenite lattice parameter and carbon content
Lattice parameter from SAED; X in wt%
Used to estimate carbon content in austenite because EDS cannot reliably measure C.

prediction.computational_details.idx_0.validation_a: Calculated yield strength contributions compared with experimental YS of S1000 (955 MPa), S1050 (945 MPa), and MHT (910 MPa); calculated precipitation strengthening contributions are similar (209, 216, 248 MPa).
prediction.computational_details.idx_0.analytical_m: σ0 = 97 + 279·C + 1.5·Mn + 20.5·Al + 2.9·Ni + 3.9·Cr (MPa, element contents in wt.%); σGR = K·deff^(-1/2) with K = 461 MPa·μm^(1/2)
Used to decompose strengthening contributions. Grain-boundary strengths: S1000 145 MPa, S1050 120 MPa, MHT 63 MPa. Solid-solution strengthening effects: S1000 601 MPa, S1050 609 MPa, MHT 599 MPa.
Calculate carbon content in austenite from lattice parameter and other element contents
Employed because EDS does not provide reliable carbon content values.

truth.unmapped_findings.idx_0.value: S1000 impact energy 29.2 ± 2.0 J; crack propagation energy 9.87 J, initiation energy ~19.33 J.
truth.unmapped_findings.idx_1.value: S1050 impact energy 42.8 ± 0.4 J; crack propagation energy 26.64 J, initiation energy ~16.16 J.
truth.unmapped_findings.idx_2.value: MHT impact energy 55.1 ± 2.2 J; crack propagation energy 36.78 J, initiation energy ~18.32 J; 88.6% higher than S1000.
truth.unmapped_findings.idx_3.value: Impact displacements: S1000 4.55 mm, S1050 6.30 mm, MHT 8.04 mm.
truth.unmapped_findings.idx_4.value: Intermediate heat treatment at 750 °C for 15 min in MHT causes grain boundary precipitation that serves as nucleation sites for twins, increasing the fraction of special boundaries and contributing to toughness.
truth.unmapped_findings.idx_5.value: Strain hardening rate curves show three stages; maximum hardening rate ~2000 MPa in true strain 0.20-0.22 for all conditions.
truth.unmapped_findings.idx_6.value: S1100 yield strength 827 MPa, ultimate tensile strength 998 MPa (used as supplementary comparison).
truth.unmapped_findings.idx_7.value: The work-hardening rate curves (Fig.4) are available but only described qualitatively in text.

prediction.unmapped_findings.idx_0.value: The strain-hardening rate of S1000, S1050, and MHT undergoes three stages: an initial sharp decrease attributed to dynamic recovery, a second stage increase reaching a maximum of 2000 MPa at a strain interval of 0.20–0.22, and a third stage decrease with increasing strain.
prediction.unmapped_findings.idx_1.value: Instrumented Charpy impact test details: S1000 crack initiation energy comparable to MHT (~18 J) and slightly higher than S1050 (~16 J); crack propagation energies are S1000 9.87 J, S1050 26.64 J, MHT 36.78 J. Impact displacements: S1000 4.55 mm, S1050 6.30 mm, MHT 8.04 mm. Note: Fig. 5(b) bar chart displays slightly different propagation energy values (S1000 ~12 J, S1050 ~26 J, MHT ~39 J) compared with the explicit text values.
prediction.unmapped_findings.idx_2.value: Cross-sectional crack propagation zone observations (Fig. 8): S1000 cracks follow random high-angle grain boundaries with aggregated micropores at precipitate-containing boundaries; S1050 cracks deflect at special boundaries producing mixed fracture; MHT exhibits zigzag crack paths crossing special boundaries with increased effective crack length and energy dissipation.
prediction.unmapped_findings.idx_3.value: The intermediate heat treatment at 750°C in MHT leads to closely spaced precipitates at grain boundaries, increasing stress in non-twinned regions and promoting a larger number of twin nucleation sites, which ultimately increases the frequency of special boundaries that interfere with random high-angle grain boundaries.
prediction.unmapped_findings.idx_4.value: Table 1 mechanical properties summary: S1000 (grain size 3.17 μm, YS 955±7 MPa, UTS 1126±3 MPa, El 55.5±1.3 %, impact 29.2±2.0 J); S1050 (grain size 3.84 μm, YS 945±14 MPa, UTS 1113±3 MPa, El 55.8±0.4 %, impact 42.8±0.4 J); MHT (grain size 7.31 μm, YS 910±7 MPa, UTS 1076±2 MPa, El 50.9±3.0 %, impact 55.1±2.2 J).
prediction.unmapped_findings.idx_5.value: S1100 (1100°C single-stage) tensile properties: yield strength 827 MPa and ultimate tensile strength 998 MPa, included as supplementary comparison in Fig. 4 but microstructure not fully characterized in the text.
prediction.unmapped_findings.idx_6.value: Toughening mechanism summary: the increased impact toughness in MHT is derived from the synergistic effect of eliminating intergranular Cr7C3 precipitates and maintaining a high proportion (~71.2%) of low-energy special grain boundaries (Σ ≤ 29), together with larger effective grain size, which increases crack propagation energy by promoting crack deflection and zigzag propagation paths.
